title: LICHEN_LINK_KEY_LEN used without including header that defines it
description:
lichen_l2.c:561: uint8_t rx_link_key[LICHEN_LINK_KEY_LEN];

The comment says 'from <lichen/link_ctx.h>' but the include at line 32 is conditional on CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK. The entire block (lines 528-592) is also guarded by #if HAVE_LICHEN_LINK.

So this is correct - the constant is only used when the header is included.

However, the code pattern of using a constant from a conditionally-included header is fragile. A refactor could move the rx_link_key declaration outside the HAVE_LICHEN_LINK block, causing a cryptic compile error.

Fix: Add a comment near the include noting which symbols are required from it, or use a local #define as a compile-time alias that fails clearly if the header isn't included.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation at the conditional include block (lines 40-49) listing all required symbols from each header, including LICHEN_LINK_KEY_LEN. The comment explicitly states these symbols must stay inside HAVE_LICHEN_LINK guards, preventing accidental misuse during refactoring.
